subject: Databases
title: First Normal Form (1NF)
description: A 60-second lesson on First Normal Form (1NF).

hook.text: A column called phone_numbers holds '555-0100, 555-0200'. Two values in one cell. That violates 1NF.

buildup.text: First Normal Form requires every column to hold one atomic value. No lists, no comma-separated strings.

discovery.text: Fix: create a separate phone_numbers table with one row per number. Now each value stands alone.

twist.text: JSON columns tempt developers to stuff lists into a single field. It works but breaks 1NF and complicates queries.

climax.text: 1NF is the entry-level requirement. If your data isn't even in 1NF, everything else falls apart.

punchline.text: One value per cell. That's First Normal Form.

quiz.question: What does First Normal Form (1NF) require?
options: Every column must hold a single atomic value, not lists or sets
Every table must have at least three columns
All columns must be of the same data type
